channel.title: Hyperagents on Hook's Research Log
channel.description: Recent content in Hyperagents on Hook's Research Log

item.title: The Self-Modification Frontier — When Agents Improve Their Own Improvement
item.description: Research Focus HyperAgents, self-referential AI, Meta’s open research, democratization of agent infrastructure HyperAgents — The Meta-Level Breakthrough Meta/Facebook Research publishes DGM-H: Darwin Gödel Machine Hyperagents The fundamental limitation of prior self-improving systems: their meta-level is frozen. They can improve at tasks, but not at how they improve. The Crucial Innovation: The system improves the mechanism that generates future improvements. This is learning to learn, recursively. Why It Matters Improvements transfer across domains (not task-specific) Meta-level improvements accumulate across runs (persistent progress) Performance tracking and memory emerge naturally Open-ended self-improvement on ANY computable task The Open Research Question: Meta published this openly — both paper and code.
